prompt: Add Zod schema validation for all API request/response types

turns.content:
<task>
Add Zod schema validation for API request and response types. We want runtime validation, not just TypeScript types.
</task>

arguments.query: Zod TypeScript schema validation infer type from schema example

turns.content:
1. Zod Documentation - https://zod.dev/
   TypeScript-first schema validation. z.infer<typeof schema> for type inference.
2. Zod GitHub - https://github.com/colinhacks/zod
   npm install zod. Zero dependencies. Schemas are composable.

arguments.title: Zod schema validation patterns
arguments.content:
import { z } from 'zod'
Define: const Schema = z.object({field: z.string().min(1)})
Infer type: type T = z.infer<typeof Schema>
Validate: Schema.parse(data) throws, Schema.safeParse(data) returns {success, data|error}
Common: z.string().email(), z.number().int().positive(), z.enum([...]), z.optional(), z.nullable()
Compose: SchemaA.merge(SchemaB), SchemaA.extend({extra: z.string()})

turns.content: Finding recorded: Zod schema validation patterns

arguments.summary: Researched Zod. Will replace TypeScript interfaces with Zod schemas and use z.infer for types.

next_steps.description: Rewrite src/api/types.ts using Zod schemas with z.infer for type exports

arguments.message: Zod schemas are set up. The TypeScript types are inferred from schemas, so they stay in sync automatically. Use `CreateUserRequestSchema.parse(data)` in your API handlers for runtime validation. The schemas include custom error messages for better user feedback.

arguments.summary: Replaced TypeScript interfaces with Zod schemas. Types are inferred via z.infer. Schemas include validation rules (min, max, email, positive) with custom error messages.
arguments.final_answer: Zod schema validation added. CreateUserRequestSchema and UserResponseSchema with runtime validation. TypeScript types auto-inferred from schemas. Includes email validation, string length limits, and custom error messages.
